basics.name: Xiangyu Shi
basics.label: Ph.D. Student
basics.summary: Ph.D. student at KTH Royal Institute of Technology, working on Graph Representation Learning, Graph Neural Networks, and distributed systems. Founding Engineer at OrbDB Labs.

work.name: Eigent AI
highlights: Maintained and extended large-scale LLM agent frameworks (CAMEL (16k+★) and Eigent (12k+★)), widely used by the open-source community, focusing on agent orchestration, memory, and tool use.
Reviewed PRs, fixed bugs, and improved code quality and system stability.

work.name: OrbDB Labs AB
highlights: Co-founded Orb DB, a startup building a hybrid database engine that integrates Graph ML inference into query execution with built-in uncertainty estimation.
Delivered the first PoC that drove substantial production improvements for a medium-scale industry partner, leading to follow-on funding.

work.name: Scania AB
highlights: Developed an end-to-end anomaly detection pipeline for vehicle sensor data with Graph Neural Networks.
Implemented counterfactual reasoning algorithms to provide explainable AI (XAI) features, reducing root-cause diagnosis time for engineering teams.

work.name: Chinese University of Hong Kong, Shenzhen
highlights: Implemented a feature extraction approach that uses audio compression in voice replay attack detection. Achieved state-of-the-art performance in the ASVspoof 2021 dataset.

work.name: Harbin Institute of Technology
highlights: Developed AutoMC, an open-source automated knowledge-graph–guided progressive search compression framework, which can achieve 70% model size reduction with zero accuracy drop.
Implemented multi-objective hyper-parameter optimization for Federated Learning, achieving the balance between performance and fairness.
